findings.title: cart.Items does one REST round-trip per cart entry (N+1)
findings.evidence: mts/cart/cart.go:39-47 -> mts/listing/processor.go:49-58 (GetBySerial is a full Browse HTTP GET); runs on every wish add/remove re-push (kafka/consumer/mts/consumer.go:267) and every purchase (removeCartWishForPurchase)
findings.scenario: A cart of N favorites triggers N sequential atlas-mts HTTP calls on unrelated ops (e.g. any purchase), serialized on the consumer goroutine.

findings.title: Wanted tab (section 2) ignores the categorySub inventory sub-tab filter
findings.evidence: socket/handler/itc_operation.go:775-779 calls mtswanted.WorldItems (mts/wanted/wanted.go:22) which takes no categorySub; sibling wishItems (itc_operation.go:876-892) and the public browse do filter.
findings.scenario: Player on the Wanted tab selects the Equip sub-tab and still sees use/setup/etc want-ads.

findings.title: Register-wish re-push navigates poster to the cross-character Wanted tab
findings.evidence: kafka/consumer/mts/consumer.go:664-666 + wishSectionForOrigin:309-318 send a section-2 GetItcListDone for RegisterWish, unlike the CANCEL_WISH fix (:702-709) which uses announceOwnWantAds.
findings.scenario: After posting a want-ad the client is yanked to the Wanted tab, which excludes the poster's own new ad.

findings.title: MTS config cache never invalidates (no TTL, restart required)
findings.evidence: mts/configuration/registry.go:61-83 caches per-tenant Model for process lifetime.
findings.scenario: Tenant edits MinLevel/fees in atlas-tenants; channel keeps old values until pod restart.

findings.title: DOM-25 gray area: section/category tab numerals are literal wire values
findings.evidence: socket/handler/itc_operation.go:292-295, :301; literal Category "1"/"3" in mts_entry.go:143 and consumer.go:200. Dispatcher mode bytes ARE config-resolved (resolveItcOperationKey).
findings.scenario: Version-dependent tab numbering would require a code change rather than a tenant-table edit; likely acceptable (client-echoed, IDA-uniform).

findings.title: emitRemoveCartWishByListingSerial header doc contradicts implementation
findings.evidence: socket/handler/itc_operation.go:931-938 (header claims listing->item lookup) vs :942-961 (matches cart wish directly by ListingSerial).
findings.scenario: Doc-only; implementation is the correct behavior.

findings.title: Auction-settle contract date uses time.Now() not the listing EndsAt
findings.scenario: Cosmetic: contract date shown to both parties is a few ms off the true settle instant.

checklistNotes.DOM-01_02_03: N/A - channel-side REST read-projection packages (RestModel+Extract, immutable Model); no GORM entity/write side.
checklistNotes.DOM-11_EXT-01: pass - lazy SliceProvider; JSON:API relationship stubs present (wish/rest.go:33-34, listing/rest.go:74-75).
checklistNotes.DOM-12: pass - no os.Getenv in MTS handlers.
checklistNotes.DOM-14_15_16: pass - zero db.Create/Save/Delete in handlers; writes via Kafka command processor.
checklistNotes.DOM-24: N/A - MTS tests exercise pure functions only; no emit path driven, no producertest needed.
checklistNotes.DOM-25: pass on dispatch axis (mode bytes + noticeFailReasons config-resolved); see M-3.
checklistNotes.multitenancy: pass - tenant.MustFromContext + t.Is(sc.Tenant()) gate in every consumer announce; ctx-scoped session/REST.
checklistNotes.immutability: pass - private fields + getters, no setters, constructed via Extract only.
checklistNotes.mockParity: N/A - mts Processor interfaces are not mocked anywhere; build/test green.
